hypothesis: ADEQUATE status is FALSE indicator of defense quality: >=2 ADEQUATE FMs have recurrence evidence
method: Systematic audit of all 13 ADEQUATE-status FMs against git log, SESSION-LOG, and lesson archive for post-ADEQUATE recurrence or near-miss incidents
expected: >=2 ADEQUATE FMs have recurrence at N>=5 concurrency. Swiss Cheese model gives false confidence.
actual: 5/13 ADEQUATE FMs (38%) have confirmed recurrence or bypass paths. FM-01: S427 cross-session bypass (3033 files deleted). FM-04: S476/S478/S480 index corruption recurrence. FM-09: detection works but no automated recovery. FM-14: WSL incidents continued post-ADEQUATE. FM-19: defense itself generates false positives (S470/S472). Additionally 1 documentation error: FM-30 marked ADEQUATE but cascade_monitor.py reclassification overcounted layers.
diff: Expected >=2 recurrences, found 5 (150% above prediction). Root mechanism: Swiss Cheese assumes INDEPENDENT defense layers, but at N>=5 concurrency, per-session guards share mutable state (git index, working tree) — layers become CORRELATED. Only creation-time structural enforcement (like FM-22's open_lane.py gate) or repo-level atomic operations prevent true recurrence.
verdict: FALSIFIED — ADEQUATE status has 38% recurrence rate at N>=5. Swiss Cheese independent-layer assumption violated by shared mutable state.
falsification_target: Swiss Cheese model: ADEQUATE (>=2 independent automated layers) = adequate defense
falsification_result: FALSIFIED — layers are not independent at high concurrency

recurrence_details.incident: S427 mass deletion by concurrent session
recurrence_details.bypass: cross-session staging not blocked by per-session guard
pre-commit hook HEAD race at N>=5

recurrence_details.incident: S427 detection worked but recovery manual

recurrence_details.bypass: filesystem-level failure outside git guard scope
defense became friction point requiring secondary fixes
